description: ATA Azure-to-AWS mapping catalog — comprehensive coverage

mappings.rationale: Direct IaaS VM equivalent
mappings.migrationNotes: Review instance type mapping; consider Graviton for cost optimization

mappings.rationale: VMSS maps to ASG + Launch Template pattern for elastic compute
mappings.migrationNotes: Configure scaling policies; review health check settings

mappings.rationale: Availability Sets map to EC2 placement groups or multi-AZ deployments
mappings.migrationNotes: Use placement groups (spread) for fault isolation; deploy across multiple AZs

mappings.rationale: Maps to EC2 Placement Groups (cluster strategy) for low-latency
mappings.migrationNotes: Use cluster placement group for low-latency between instances

mappings.rationale: Custom VM images map to AMIs (Amazon Machine Images)
mappings.migrationNotes: Use VM Import/Export to convert VHD to AMI; review image builder pipelines
alternatives: EC2 Image Builder (for automated image pipelines)

mappings.rationale: Direct disk snapshot equivalent
mappings.migrationNotes: Map snapshot lifecycle policies; review cross-region copy settings

mappings.rationale: Shared Image Gallery maps to AMI sharing across accounts/regions
mappings.migrationNotes: Configure AMI sharing permissions; use EC2 Image Builder for versioning

mappings.rationale: App Service web apps map to App Runner for managed web hosting; Elastic Beanstalk is also viable
mappings.migrationNotes: Review deployment model; App Runner is simpler but less configurable than Beanstalk

mappings.rationale: Azure Functions and Lambda are direct serverless function equivalents
mappings.migrationNotes: Review trigger types; map bindings to AWS event sources (API Gateway, SQS, EventBridge)

mappings.rationale: App Service Plans define compute for web apps; maps to App Runner or ECS compute
mappings.migrationNotes: App Service Plan tiers map to instance sizing; review auto-scaling settings

mappings.rationale: Azure Static Web Apps and Amplify Hosting both serve JAMstack/SPA apps
mappings.migrationNotes: Review custom domain and authentication settings; migrate CI/CD pipeline

mappings.rationale: Direct managed batch computing equivalent
mappings.migrationNotes: Map pool configurations to compute environments; review job definitions
alternatives: AWS Step Functions + Lambda (for serverless batch)

mappings.rationale: AKS and EKS are both managed Kubernetes services
mappings.migrationNotes: Review node pool config, networking (VPC CNI), and IAM for service accounts (IRSA)
alternatives: Amazon ECS (if simplifying away from K8s)

mappings.rationale: Direct container registry equivalent
mappings.migrationNotes: Migrate images; configure lifecycle policies

mappings.rationale: ACI and Fargate both provide serverless container execution
mappings.migrationNotes: Review resource limits (CPU/memory); map to ECS task definitions with Fargate launch type
alternatives: AWS App Runner (for simpler web containers)

mappings.rationale: Container Apps and App Runner both provide managed container hosting with auto-scaling
mappings.migrationNotes: Review scaling rules, ingress settings, and Dapr sidecar usage
alternatives: Amazon ECS on Fargate (for more control)

mappings.rationale: Azure Blob Storage and S3 are direct object storage equivalents
mappings.migrationNotes: Map access tiers (Hot/Cool/Archive → S3 Standard/IA/Glacier); review lifecycle rules

mappings.rationale: General-purpose v2 storage accounts primarily use blob; maps to S3
mappings.migrationNotes: If using File shares, consider Amazon EFS; if using Table/Queue, see DynamoDB/SQS

mappings.rationale: Azure Files maps to Amazon EFS for managed NFS file shares
mappings.migrationNotes: Review performance tier (Standard/Premium → EFS Bursting/Provisioned); map SMB → NFS if needed
alternatives: Amazon FSx for Windows File Server (for SMB/AD integration)

mappings.rationale: Storage account without specific kind defaults to S3 mapping; actual usage may vary
mappings.migrationNotes: Inspect actual usage (blob/file/queue/table) to refine mapping

mappings.rationale: Managed disks map directly to EBS volumes
mappings.migrationNotes: Map disk types (Premium SSD → gp3/io2, Standard HDD → st1/sc1)

mappings.rationale: Azure NetApp Files maps to Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP
mappings.migrationNotes: Review volume performance tiers and snapshot policies

mappings.rationale: Recovery Services Vault maps to AWS Backup for centralized backup management
mappings.migrationNotes: Map backup policies, retention periods, and vault lock settings

mappings.rationale: Direct virtual network equivalent
mappings.migrationNotes: Map subnets, CIDR ranges, and peering connections; review DNS settings

mappings.rationale: VNet subnets map directly to VPC subnets
mappings.migrationNotes: Map CIDR ranges; configure route tables and NACLs per subnet

mappings.rationale: NSGs map to Security Groups; note AWS also has NACLs for subnet-level filtering
mappings.migrationNotes: NSG rules → SG rules; consider NACLs for subnet-level deny rules (NSG supports deny, SG does not)

mappings.rationale: App Gateway combines L7 LB + WAF; AWS splits these into ALB + WAF
mappings.migrationNotes: Configure ALB listeners/rules; attach WAF WebACL if using WAF features
alternatives: Amazon CloudFront + WAF (for CDN + WAF pattern)

mappings.rationale: Azure LB (Standard) maps to NLB for L4 load balancing
mappings.migrationNotes: Review health check config and cross-zone settings
alternatives: Application Load Balancer (if L7 features needed)

mappings.rationale: Direct VPN gateway equivalent for site-to-site connectivity
mappings.migrationNotes: Configure Customer Gateway + Virtual Private Gateway; review BGP settings

mappings.rationale: ExpressRoute and Direct Connect are both dedicated private connectivity services
mappings.migrationNotes: Coordinate with connectivity provider; review bandwidth and redundancy requirements
alternatives: AWS Site-to-Site VPN (as interim or backup)

mappings.rationale: Private Endpoints and PrivateLink both provide private connectivity to services
mappings.migrationNotes: Configure VPC endpoint for each service; review DNS resolution (Route 53 Resolver)

mappings.rationale: Public IP addresses map to Elastic IPs for static public addressing
mappings.migrationNotes: Elastic IPs are per-region; review association with EC2/NAT/ELB

mappings.rationale: Direct network interface equivalent
mappings.migrationNotes: ENIs are automatically created with EC2 instances; review secondary IP assignments

mappings.rationale: Direct NAT gateway equivalent for outbound internet from private subnets
mappings.migrationNotes: Deploy per-AZ for high availability; review Elastic IP association
alternatives: NAT Instance (for cost savings in dev/test)

mappings.rationale: Direct route table equivalent
mappings.migrationNotes: Map custom routes; review route propagation from VPN/Direct Connect

mappings.rationale: Network Watcher provides flow logs and diagnostics; maps to VPC Flow Logs
mappings.migrationNotes: Enable VPC Flow Logs for traffic monitoring; use CloudWatch for analysis

mappings.rationale: Azure Firewall policies map to AWS Network Firewall rule groups
mappings.migrationNotes: Map DNAT/SNAT rules; review threat intelligence feed integration

mappings.rationale: Azure Firewall and AWS Network Firewall are both managed network firewalls
mappings.migrationNotes: Map application rules, network rules, and DNAT rules to Suricata-compatible rules

mappings.rationale: Azure Front Door combines CDN + WAF + global load balancing; maps to CloudFront + WAF
mappings.migrationNotes: Configure CloudFront distributions with WAF WebACL; use Route 53 for routing policies

mappings.rationale: Direct DNS zone equivalent
mappings.migrationNotes: Export zone file and import to Route 53; update NS records at registrar

mappings.rationale: Private DNS zones map to Route 53 Private Hosted Zones
mappings.migrationNotes: Associate with VPCs; configure Route 53 Resolver for hybrid DNS

mappings.rationale: Traffic Manager DNS routing maps to Route 53 routing policies (weighted, latency, failover)
mappings.migrationNotes: Map routing method to Route 53 policy type; configure health checks

mappings.rationale: Direct CDN equivalent
mappings.migrationNotes: Map origins, caching rules, and custom domains; review SSL certificate setup

mappings.rationale: Both are managed API gateway services with throttling, auth, and transformation
mappings.migrationNotes: Map API policies to API Gateway stages/usage plans; review OAuth/JWT validation

mappings.rationale: Azure SQL logical server maps to RDS SQL Server
mappings.migrationNotes: Review edition/tier mapping; consider Aurora PostgreSQL if open to engine change

mappings.rationale: Azure SQL Database maps to RDS SQL Server instance
mappings.migrationNotes: Use AWS DMS for migration; map DTU/vCore to RDS instance class

mappings.rationale: SQL Managed Instance maps to RDS SQL Server with near-complete SQL Server compatibility
mappings.migrationNotes: Review SQL Agent jobs, linked servers, CLR features; use DMS for data migration

mappings.rationale: Elastic pools share resources across databases; AWS uses individual RDS instances
mappings.migrationNotes: Split into separate RDS instances; consider Aurora Serverless for variable workloads

mappings.rationale: Cosmos DB (SQL API) maps to DynamoDB; data model differences exist
mappings.migrationNotes: Review consistency models; Cosmos DB multi-model vs DynamoDB key-value/document

mappings.rationale: Direct managed Redis equivalent
mappings.migrationNotes: Map tier/size; review cluster mode and replication settings
alternatives: Amazon MemoryDB for Redis (for durable Redis)

mappings.rationale: Direct managed PostgreSQL equivalent
mappings.migrationNotes: Use AWS DMS for migration; consider Aurora PostgreSQL for higher performance

mappings.rationale: Single-server PostgreSQL maps to RDS PostgreSQL
mappings.migrationNotes: Single-server is deprecated; migrate to flexible server equivalent on RDS

mappings.rationale: Direct managed MySQL equivalent
mappings.migrationNotes: Use AWS DMS for migration; consider Aurora MySQL for higher performance

mappings.rationale: Direct managed MariaDB equivalent
mappings.migrationNotes: Use DMS for data migration; review storage engine settings

mappings.rationale: Azure Cognitive Search maps to OpenSearch Service for full-text search
mappings.migrationNotes: Review index schemas and analyzers; map scoring profiles to OpenSearch queries

mappings.rationale: Service Bus queues → SQS, topics → SNS; combined pattern for pub/sub + queuing
mappings.migrationNotes: If using sessions or AMQP, consider Amazon MQ instead; review dead-letter queue config

mappings.rationale: Event Hubs and Kinesis are both high-throughput event streaming services
mappings.migrationNotes: Map partition count and throughput units; review consumer group patterns

mappings.rationale: Event Grid and EventBridge are both serverless event routing services
mappings.migrationNotes: Map event subscriptions to EventBridge rules; review filtering patterns

mappings.rationale: SignalR maps to API Gateway WebSocket APIs for real-time messaging
mappings.migrationNotes: Use API Gateway WebSocket with Lambda backend; review connection management

mappings.rationale: Logic Apps and Step Functions are both serverless workflow orchestration services
mappings.migrationNotes: Map connectors to AWS SDK integrations; review error handling and retry patterns

mappings.rationale: Data Factory pipelines map to Step Functions for ETL orchestration
mappings.migrationNotes: Map activities to Lambda functions or Glue jobs; review data source connectors

mappings.rationale: Key Vault combines secrets + keys + certificates; AWS splits into Secrets Manager (secrets) + KMS (keys) + ACM (certs)
mappings.migrationNotes: Map secrets → Secrets Manager, encryption keys → KMS, certificates → ACM
alternatives: AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store (for simpler secret storage)

mappings.rationale: Managed identities map to IAM Roles for service-to-service authentication
mappings.migrationNotes: Create IAM roles with appropriate trust policies; use IRSA for EKS workloads

mappings.rationale: Azure RBAC role assignments map to IAM policy attachments
mappings.migrationNotes: Map Azure roles to AWS managed policies; review least-privilege principle

mappings.rationale: Defender for Cloud maps to Security Hub for centralized security management
mappings.migrationNotes: Enable Security Hub standards (CIS, PCI); configure automated findings

mappings.rationale: WAF policies map directly to AWS WAF WebACLs
mappings.migrationNotes: Map custom rules and managed rule sets; attach to ALB or CloudFront

mappings.rationale: Application Insights maps to CloudWatch (metrics/logs) + X-Ray (distributed tracing)
mappings.migrationNotes: Instrument with CloudWatch SDK and X-Ray SDK; review custom metrics and alerts
alternatives: Amazon Managed Grafana + Prometheus (for open-source observability)

mappings.rationale: Log Analytics workspace maps to CloudWatch Logs for centralized log management
mappings.migrationNotes: Migrate KQL queries to CloudWatch Logs Insights syntax
alternatives: Amazon OpenSearch Service (for advanced log analytics)

mappings.rationale: Action Groups map to SNS topics for alert notifications
mappings.migrationNotes: Map notification channels (email, SMS, webhook) to SNS subscriptions

mappings.rationale: Metric alerts map directly to CloudWatch Alarms
mappings.migrationNotes: Map threshold conditions and evaluation periods; configure SNS for notifications

mappings.rationale: Activity Log alerts map to EventBridge rules on CloudTrail events
mappings.migrationNotes: Create EventBridge rules to match specific API calls; target SNS or Lambda

mappings.rationale: Diagnostic settings enable logging/metrics; maps to CloudWatch metric streams and log groups
mappings.migrationNotes: Enable CloudWatch agent on EC2; configure log groups and metric filters

mappings.rationale: Automation Accounts map to Systems Manager for runbook automation and patching
mappings.migrationNotes: Map runbooks to SSM documents; configure Maintenance Windows for patching

mappings.rationale: Azure Advisor and Trusted Advisor both provide best-practice recommendations
mappings.migrationNotes: Review cost optimization, security, and performance recommendations

mappings.rationale: Cost Management exports map to Cost Explorer reports and CUR (Cost and Usage Report)
mappings.migrationNotes: Set up CUR in S3; use Cost Explorer for visualization and budgets

mappings.rationale: Azure Resource Groups map to AWS Resource Groups + Tags for organization
mappings.migrationNotes: Define tagging strategy; use Resource Groups for logical grouping

mappings.rationale: Azure Portal dashboards map to CloudWatch Dashboards
mappings.migrationNotes: Recreate dashboard widgets; use CloudWatch metrics/logs as data sources

mappings.rationale: Azure AI Services map to Bedrock and various AWS AI services
mappings.migrationNotes: Map specific cognitive APIs to AWS equivalents (Textract, Rekognition, Comprehend, etc.)

mappings.rationale: Azure ML workspaces map to SageMaker for end-to-end ML lifecycle
mappings.migrationNotes: Map compute instances, experiments, and model registry; review MLOps pipelines

mappings.rationale: Notification Hubs maps to Pinpoint for push notifications across platforms
mappings.migrationNotes: Register device tokens; map templates and targeting rules

mappings.rationale: Auto-shutdown schedules map to Instance Scheduler for cost optimization
mappings.migrationNotes: Deploy Instance Scheduler solution; configure start/stop schedules via tags
alternatives: AWS Systems Manager Session Manager (for ad-hoc management)

mappings.rationale: App Service Certificates map to ACM for SSL/TLS certificate management
mappings.migrationNotes: Request or import certificates in ACM; associate with ALB/CloudFront

mappings.rationale: Smart detection maps to CloudWatch Anomaly Detection for ML-based alerting
mappings.migrationNotes: Enable anomaly detection on key metrics; review sensitivity thresholds
alternatives: Amazon DevOps Guru (for ML-powered operational insights)
